sections.paragraph:
ξένος, η, ον [etym. complex] ‘relating to what is normally outside one’s immediate experience’

—a. strange, unusual Ac 17:18 (perh. ‘foreign’); Hb 13:9; 1 Pt 4:12; Eph 2:12 (ξ. τῶν διαϑηϰῶν,outside the covenants).

—b. as noun ὁ ξένος stranger Mt 25:35; 27:7; Ac 17:21; Eph 2:19; Hb 11:13; 3 J 5. With focus on the dispenser of hospitality host, an apparent shift in the mng. of ὁ ξένος made possible by the fact that the host and the beneficiary are in a reciprocal relationship of relative ‘strangeness’ Ro 16:23.
